title: Chapter 93
content:
Chapter 93: 93 | The Arrogant Princess and Her Indecent Proposal

I stepped inside.

The first thing that hit me was the scale of it. This wasn’t an apartment. This was a fucking penthouse.

The entrance hallway alone was bigger than my entire living room back at 5E. Hardwood floors that actually looked expensive, not the engineered laminate we had in the dorms. Crown molding along the ceiling. Recessed lighting that created this soft ambient glow instead of harsh overhead fluorescents.

We walked past a sitting area with leather furniture that probably cost more than my dad’s car. Floor-to-ceiling windows showed the ocean stretched out in every direction, the sunset painting everything orange and gold.

A full kitchen sat off to the left. Not the little kitchenette with two burners and a mini-fridge. An actual kitchen with granite countertops, stainless steel appliances, and a gas range that looked professional-grade.

"Jesus," I said.

Misato didn’t respond. Just kept walking.

We passed what looked like a home gym. Weights. Machines. A treadmill facing another ocean view window.

Two staircases. One going up, one going down.

How many floors did this place have?

My brain tried to calculate the square footage and gave up around three thousand.

Misato and Blair lived here. Just the two of them.

While I shared a decent but normal apartment with Hikaru. While Belle had a regular dorm room. While Naomi probably had the same setup.

The Elite Ten lived like this.

And we scraped by on stipends and vending machine snacks.

The wealth gap wasn’t a gap. It was a fucking canyon.

Misato led me into what I could only describe as a formal living room. Two cream-colored couches facing each other across a glass coffee table. Abstract art on the walls that probably cost six figures.

"Sit," Misato said.

I sat on the couch. The cushions were stupid comfortable.

Misato remained standing. Her arms crossed. Her lime green ponytail perfect despite three hours of training.

I looked around the room again. Then back at Misato.

"So where is he?" I asked.

Misato blinked. "What?"

"Blair. Where’s Blair?"

"She’s finishing her shower." Misato’s expression didn’t change. "She’ll be out in a minute."

Right.

Of course Blair lived somewhere with multiple bathrooms. Probably had a master suite upstairs with a tub the size of my bedroom.

I sat back. Tried to look casual instead of intimidated by the casual display of obscene wealth.

The silence stretched.

Water running in pipes somewhere above us. Then the distinct sound of a shower turning off.

Footsteps on tile.

A door opening.

Steam rolling out into a hallway I couldn’t see.

And then Blair Davenport walked into the living room.

I stopped breathing.

She wore a black silk robe. Short. Ending mid-thigh. The fabric clung to her wet skin in ways that defied physics. Her red hair hung damp around her face, darker when wet, water droplets sliding down her neck and disappearing beneath the silk.

The robe was tied loosely at her waist. The V of the opening showed everything from her throat down to her sternum. Black lace panties visible beneath when she moved. And the silk was thin enough that I could see the outline of her nipples through the fabric. Hardened from the cold air or the temperature shift after the shower.

Her body was obscene. That 39F bust pressed against wet silk. The tiny waist that made her proportions look like someone drew her with a ruler and went insane with the measurements. Wide hips and thick thighs that the short robe barely covered.

She looked like someone’s fantasy stepped directly out of a shower and into reality.

Blair walked across the room with her characteristic confidence. Sat down on the couch across from me. Crossed her legs slowly.

The movement made the robe ride up another inch.

I swallowed.

Blair looked directly at me. Her ice-blue eyes cold. Appraising.

"Hello, Jace."

Her voice was smooth. Controlled. The way someone speaks when they know they hold all the cards.

"I have a proposal for you."

My brain struggled to restart. Between the outfit and the words and the fact that Blair Davenport had just said my actual name, I was barely functional.

Get it together.

"A proposal," I repeated.

"Yes." Blair leaned back. The robe shifted. More skin visible. "Regarding Friday’s assessment."

"Okay."

"I want you to fail."

The words hit like cold water.

"What?"

"Fail," Blair said. Simple. Direct. "Perform poorly. Drag your squad’s ranking down. Ensure the Midnight Foxes place far below my team."

My brain finally caught up with what she was saying.

The mesmerized fog cleared. Replaced by something sharp and hot.

"Are you serious?"

"Completely." Blair’s expression didn’t change. "When your squad inevitably falls apart due to lack of leadership and poor coordination, Misato returns to where she belongs. With me."

My hands clenched on my knees.

"You want me to throw the assessment. So you can steal back your friend."

"Precisely."

"Fuck no."

Blair’s eyes narrowed. "Excuse me?"

"I said no." I leaned forward. "I’m not sabotaging my squad so you can feel better about losing your assistant."

Blair stood. Her face flushed. Not with embarrassment. With anger.

"What do you mean no?" Her voice rose. Sharp. Brittle. "Is it money? I can compensate you. Name your price."

I looked at Misato. "Really? So all that talk about building something of your own was bullshit?"

Misato’s expression flickered. Surprise. Then something else.

"I didn’t—" Misato started.

"Be quiet," Blair snapped. She stalked toward me. "You’re a lottery winner. A nobody from nowhere with zero prospects and a death sentence hanging over your head the moment you step into a real gate."

She stopped directly in front of me. Looming. Her eyes blazing.

"I’m offering you money. Real money. Enough to change your pathetic life. And you’re saying no?"

"Yeah. I am."

"Why?" Blair’s hands gestured wildly. Angry. Frustrated. "You have nothing. You are nothing. What could possibly—"

Her movements made the robe shift.

The tie came loose.

The silk fell open.

Her breasts spilled free. Pale skin. Pink nipples still hard from the shower. The 39F bust swaying slightly with her animated gestures.

Blair didn’t notice. Too angry.

"You’re a fat slob who doesn’t know shit about this academy or how power works here," she continued. Her voice getting higher. Sharper. "You think your little squad of misfits matters? You think Misato chose you? She was assigned to you. The moment she can transfer to a functional team, she will."

Blair jabbed a finger at my chest. Punctuating each word.

"You. Are. Temporary."

The robe hung completely open now. Her entire upper body exposed. The black lace panties sitting low on her hips.

She still hadn’t noticed.

"And I am offering you a way out," Blair said. "Take the money. Fail quietly. Go back to whatever hole you crawled out of and let the real hunters do their job."

Misato stepped forward. "Blair—"

"Stay out of this." Blair whirled toward her. The movement made her breasts bounce.

Holy shit.

Misato’s eyes widened. "Blair. Your robe."

Blair looked down.

Saw herself completely exposed.

Her face went from angry red to mortified white in half a second.

"You—" She grabbed the robe. Yanked it closed. Tied the belt with shaking hands. "You perverted piece of—"

"You’re the one who walked out in a wet robe," I said. "And you’re the one who started yelling without noticing it fell open."

"Get out."

"Gladly."

I stood. Headed toward the door.

"Monroe," Misato called.

I stopped. Turned back.

Misato’s expression was complicated. Guilty. Conflicted.

"I didn’t know she was going to—"

"Save it." I looked at Blair. Still clutching her robe. Her face red. Her eyes furious and humiliated. "Tell your friend I’m not interested in her money. And I’m not throwing Friday."

Blair’s voice came out cold. Venomous. "You’ll regret this."

"Probably." I shrugged. "But at least I’ll regret it on my terms."
